problem: The hx-cli now has retry logic (3 attempts with exponential backoff), 30s per-request timeout, and retries on 504/502/503/500/429. These were added after the prior run identified their absence. Despite this hardening, agents still cannot reliably access runtime logs because the upstream 504 Gateway Timeout from DigitalOcean persists across all retry attempts. The CLI's retry mechanism is working correctly but cannot recover from a persistent gateway-level issue.

files.why: Core HTTP client. NOW has: MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 (line 3), BASE_DELAY_MS=2000 (line 4), REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 with AbortSignal.timeout (line 5/72), RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES=[429,500,502,503,504] (line 6). Exponential backoff with jitter (line 24-26). HTML error page detection (line 30-31). Prior run said this was missing — it has since been added.
Log query command: resolves repo then calls hxFetch POST to /api/inspect/{repoId}/logs with {query, limit}.
Database query command: same pattern — resolve repo then hxFetch POST to /api/inspect/{repoId}/database.
Command dispatcher for inspect subcommands: repos, db, logs, api (lines 22-72).
Repo resolution: fetches full repo list via GET /api/inspect/repositories on every command. No caching.
Config loading: env vars HELIX_API_KEY/HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN/HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY for auth, HELIX_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL for server URL. File fallback: ~/.hlx/config.json.
Version 1.2.0, zero runtime deps, build: tsc, typecheck: tsc --noEmit. Node >=18.

facts: CURRENT CODE HAS RETRY LOGIC: http.ts MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, BASE_DELAY_MS=2000, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=30000, retries on 429/500/502/503/504. Prior run said this was missing — it has since been added.
AbortSignal.timeout(30000) applied to every fetch call (line 72).
Backoff formula: 2000 × 2^(attempt-1) + random(0-500)ms. Max total for persistent 504: ~96s.
HTML error pages (from DigitalOcean gateway) detected by content-type and produce user-friendly message (line 30-31).
429 (rate limit) specially handled: respects Retry-After header, capped at 60s (lines 80-88).
Each inspect command makes 2 sequential HTTP calls: resolveRepo GET then actual query POST.
Auth supports both hxi_ API keys (X-API-Key header) and JWT tokens (Authorization: Bearer).
No runtime dependencies — pure Node.js built-ins (fetch, crypto, fs).
unknowns: Whether the CLI's 30s per-attempt timeout is shorter or longer than the DigitalOcean gateway timeout — determines if CLI timeout or gateway timeout is hit first.
Whether caching repo resolution result would meaningfully reduce flakiness.
Whether the CLI should surface per-attempt timing for debugging.
